mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below 6.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, boosting consumer demand and corporate earnings, driving positive SPX returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below the 6.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS combined with stable INDPRO indicates strong credit-fueled consumption without supply bottlenecks, leading to positive earnings surprises and equity price appreciation over 60 days.
failure_condition: Joint positive RSAFS and stable INDPRO periods produce negative or zero SPX 60-day returns in more than 40% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, CPIAUCSL accelerating above 2.5% annualized signals inflation risk that compresses P/E multiples, driving negative SPX returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances despite rising CPI and rates.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential increases in HOUST reflect improving housing market sentiment and wealth effects, supporting consumer spending and equity valuations over a 5-day trading horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month increases fail to predict positive SPX 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: INDPRO dropping below 98.0 indicates manufacturing contraction, reducing corporate revenue growth expectations and driving negative SPX returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO breaches the 98.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid PAYEMS growth coupled with declining UNRATE signals a self-reinforcing labor demand shock that boosts corporate margins and equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS acceleration and UNRATE decline produce negative SPX 20-day returns in more than 45% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, rising GDPC1 fails to translate into equity gains as margin compression dominates, resulting in negative SPX returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances despite high inflation and rising GDP.
